questions.question: A ball falls from rest through the air towards the ground. The diagram shows two forces acting on the ball. air resistance gravitational force As the ball falls, the air resistance increases. Which statement is correct? A The acceleration of the ball decreases., B The acceleration of the ball increases., C The speed of the ball decreases., D The gravitational force on the ball decreases. 3
questions.text: A ball falls from rest through the air towards the ground. The diagram shows two forces acting on the ball. air resistance gravitational force As the ball falls, the air resistance increases. Which statement is correct? A The acceleration of the ball decreases., B The acceleration of the ball increases., C The speed of the ball decreases., D The gravitational force on the ball decreases. 3
options: A The acceleration of the ball decreases.
B The acceleration of the ball increases.
C The speed of the ball decreases.
D The gravitational force on the ball decreases. 3

questions.question: A compressed spring projects a ball horizontally in a vacuum chamber. On the Earth, the ball reaches the chamber floor 4.0 m in front of the spring. An  identical  experiment  is  done  on  the  Moon.  The  gravitational  field  strength  is  lower  on  the Moon than on the Earth. The experimental results on the Moon are compared with those on the Earth. Which statement is correct? A The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  4.0 m  in  front  of  the spring., B The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  more  than  4.0 m  in front of the spring., C The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor 4.0 m in front of the spring., D The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor more than 4.0 m in front of the spring.
questions.text: A compressed spring projects a ball horizontally in a vacuum chamber. On the Earth, the ball reaches the chamber floor 4.0 m in front of the spring. An  identical  experiment  is  done  on  the  Moon.  The  gravitational  field  strength  is  lower  on  the Moon than on the Earth. The experimental results on the Moon are compared with those on the Earth. Which statement is correct? A The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  4.0 m  in  front  of  the spring., B The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  more  than  4.0 m  in front of the spring., C The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor 4.0 m in front of the spring., D The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor more than 4.0 m in front of the spring.
options: A The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  4.0 m  in  front  of  the spring.
B The  horizontal  speed  is  greater  on  the  Moon  and  the  ball  hits  the  floor  more  than  4.0 m  in front of the spring.
C The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor 4.0 m in front of the spring.
D The horizontal speed is the same on the Moon and the ball hits the floor more than 4.0 m in front of the spring.

questions.question: An air pistol fires a pellet forwards. What is the motion of the air pistol? A The air pistol moves backwards with speed greater than the pellet., B The air pistol moves backwards with speed less than the pellet., C The air pistol moves forward with speed greater than the pellet., D The air pistol moves forward with speed less than the pellet.

A The air pistol moves backwards with speed greater than the pellet., B The air pistol moves backwards with speed less than the pellet., C The air pistol moves forward with speed greater than the pellet., D The air pistol moves forward with speed less than the pellet.
options: A The air pistol moves backwards with speed greater than the pellet.
B The air pistol moves backwards with speed less than the pellet.
C The air pistol moves forward with speed greater than the pellet.
D The air pistol moves forward with speed less than the pellet.

questions.question: When pollen grains in water are viewed through a microscope, they are seen to be in continuous, rapid random motion. What causes a pollen grain to move in this way? A convection currents in the water, B bombardment by a single molecule of water, C uneven bombardment on different sides by water molecules, D collision with another pollen grain due to their kinetic energies 9

B bombardment by a single molecule of water

D collision with another pollen grain due to their kinetic energies 9

questions.question: Metals are good thermal conductors. Insulators are poor thermal conductors. Which description of the mechanism of thermal conductivity is correct? A In insulators, conduction takes place by electron transfer and molecular vibrations., B In insulators, conduction takes place by electron transfer only., C In metals, conduction takes place by electron transfer and molecular vibrations., D In metals, conduction takes place by electron transfer only. 11

options: A In insulators, conduction takes place by electron transfer and molecular vibrations.
B In insulators, conduction takes place by electron transfer only.
C In metals, conduction takes place by electron transfer and molecular vibrations.
D In metals, conduction takes place by electron transfer only. 11

questions.question: A teacher shows his class a polystyrene cup. The cup is made from thick plastic with lots of tiny air bubbles in it. He  asks  the  class  why  the  cup  is  so  good  at  keeping  a  hot  drink  warm.  Three  suggestions  are made. 1     It contains air which is a poor thermal conductor. 2     The air is trapped in tiny bubbles so very little convection is possible. 3     The plastic is a poor thermal conductor. Which suggestions are correct? A 1 and 2 only B    1 and 3 only C    2 and 3 only D    1, 2 and 3
questions.text: A teacher shows his class a polystyrene cup. The cup is made from thick plastic with lots of tiny air bubbles in it. options: A 1 and 2 only B    1 and 3 only C    2 and 3 only D    1, 2 and 3

questions.question: A boy jumps into an indoor swimming pool. He notices that the water appears to get colder as he goes deeper underwater. This is due to convection. Which statement is correct? A Cold water is more dense than warm water so it sinks to the bottom of the pool., B Warm water is more dense than cold water so it rises to the surface of the pool., C The molecules in cold water have more kinetic energy than the molecules in warm water so they move to the bottom of the pool faster., D The  molecules  in  warm  water  are  closer  together  than  the  molecules  in  cold  water  so  they rise to the surface of the pool.
questions.text: A boy jumps into an indoor swimming pool. He notices that the water appears to get colder as he goes deeper underwater. This is due to convection. Which statement is correct? A Cold water is more dense than warm water so it sinks to the bottom of the pool., B Warm water is more dense than cold water so it rises to the surface of the pool., C The molecules in cold water have more kinetic energy than the molecules in warm water so they move to the bottom of the pool faster., D The  molecules  in  warm  water  are  closer  together  than  the  molecules  in  cold  water  so  they rise to the surface of the pool.
options: A Cold water is more dense than warm water so it sinks to the bottom of the pool.
B Warm water is more dense than cold water so it rises to the surface of the pool.
C The molecules in cold water have more kinetic energy than the molecules in warm water so they move to the bottom of the pool faster.
D The  molecules  in  warm  water  are  closer  together  than  the  molecules  in  cold  water  so  they rise to the surface of the pool.

questions.question: When Rutherford bombarded thin gold foil with α-particles, he found that some α-particles were deflected through large angles. Which statement explains this deflection? A Most of the atom consists of empty space., B All of the positive charge and most of the mass of the gold atom are concentrated in a small volume., C Positive charge in the gold atom is spread evenly throughout the atom., D All of the negative charge is concentrated at its centre. 20 Permission  to  reproduce  items  where  third-party  owned  material  protected  by  copyright  is  included  has  been  sought  and  cleared  where  possible. questions.question: The  diagram  shows  the  path  followed  by  α-particles  as  they  pass  between  two  charged  plates. They are deflected downwards. +++++++++ ––––––––– α-particles β-particles What happens to β-particles passing through the same electric field? A They are deflected downwards more than the α-particles., B They are deflected upwards., C They are not deflected at all., D They are deflected downwards by the same amount as the α-particles.
questions.text: The  diagram  shows  the  path  followed  by  α-particles  as  they  pass  between  two  charged  plates. They are deflected downwards. +++++++++ ––––––––– α-particles β-particles What happens to β-particles passing through the same electric field? A They are deflected downwards more than the α-particles., B They are deflected upwards., C They are not deflected at all., D They are deflected downwards by the same amount as the α-particles.
options: A They are deflected downwards more than the α-particles.
C They are not deflected at all.
D They are deflected downwards by the same amount as the α-particles.

questions.question: The graph shows the count rate from a radioactive source over a period of time. 2000 1500 1000 500 0 0123 time / hours count rate counts / s What is the half-life of the source? A 0.5    hour    B    1.0    hour    C    1.5    hours    D    3.0    hours
